info.name: iGOT Deterministic Chatbot API
info.description:
## Quick setup

1. Set collection variables: `base_url`, `auth_token`, and `kong_token`.
2. **Local dev:** set `AUTH_DISABLED=true` in `.env`, use any UUID as `auth_token`, leave `kong_token` empty.
3. **Dev / UAT (Kong):** set `base_url` to `https://portal.dev.karmayogibharat.net/api`, paste Keycloak JWT as `auth_token`, paste Kong JWT as `kong_token`.

Two auth headers are required when calling through Kong:
- `x-authenticated-user-token` — Keycloak JWT (identifies the user)
- `Authorization: Bearer` — Kong JWT (authorizes API access)

## Typical flow
```
GET  /sessions/list              → session found? go to history : start fresh
GET  /sessions/history/{id}      → messages non-empty? resume : start fresh
POST /sessions/create                   → start fresh (get session_id, auto-saved)
POST /sessions/turn/{id}         → repeat until response has type:end
```

## Activity type → next action mapping

Every `/turn` response has `activities[]`. Each entry has a `type` field:

| Response activity type | What to render | Next action to send |
|---|---|---|
| `markdown` | Chat bubble with Markdown | Nothing — render and wait for the next activity |
| `quick_replies` | Row of tappable buttons | `select_choice` with `choice_id` from `choices[].id` |
| `picker` | Searchable scrollable list | `pick_item` with `picker_id`, `item_id`, `item_label` from the picker |
| `nested_picker` | Grouped accordion list (plans → courses) | `pick_item` with `picker_id`, and `item_id`/`item_label` from the **child** item tapped (not the group header) |
| `input` | Free-text input box | `send_message` with `text` = what the user typed |
| `end` | End-of-conversation banner | Nothing — session is over |
| `text` | Plain text bubble (no Markdown) | Only sent on **session expiry** — call `POST /sessions/create` to restart |
| `typing` | Loading animation | Not emitted by any current flow — reserved for future streaming |

**Multiple activities in one response:** render all of them top-to-bottom, then send ONE action in reply to the last interactive one (quick_replies / picker / input). markdown and typing activities don't need a reply on their own.

variable.description: Auto-filled by the Start Session test script.
Keycloak JWT (x-authenticated-user-token). Local dev (AUTH_DISABLED=true): any UUID. Dev/UAT/Prod: real Keycloak JWT.
Kong API gateway JWT. Required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod). Leave empty for local dev.

item.name: 1 — API Reference
item.description: All available endpoints, one each, in the order they are called. Use these as a reference or to test individual calls.

request.description:
Liveness probe. No auth required.

Expected: `{"status": "ok"}`

exec: // If an active session exists, save it so the next requests use it automatically
console.log('Active session found → session_id saved:', r.session_id);
console.log('No active session → run POST /sessions/create/create next');

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Call this first on every app open.

**Response — session found:**
```json
{ "session_id": "<uuid>", "status": "in_flow", "flow_id": "AUTO_LOGOUT_CRASH" }
```
→ Call GET /sessions/history/{id} next.
- messages[] always non-empty → render thread, last role:bot entry = current prompt
  (if user opened but hadn't picked a topic yet, history returns the initial greeting + category menu)

**Response — no active session:**
```json
{ "session_id": null }
```
→ Call POST /sessions/create to start fresh.

Always returns 200 — never errors. Returns null when Redis is unavailable (safe fallback).

exec: // Save session_id — every subsequent turn request needs it
console.log('New session started → session_id saved:', r.session_id);

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Creates a new chat session. Call this when GET /sessions/list returns null.

`channel`: `web` | `mobile` | `whatsapp` | `voice`
`language`: `en` | `hi` | any BCP-47 code

**Response contains:**
- `session_id` — save this, used in every subsequent request (auto-saved by test script)
- `activities[]` — greeting message + topic picker (14 topics as quick_reply buttons)
- `status: "awaiting_user"`

Next: call POST /sessions/create/turn/{session_id} with the topic the user picks.

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
WHY: Returns every user action and bot response from the start of the session — so the UI can render a full scrollable chat thread, not just the last bot message.

{{session_id}} — from POST /sessions/create/create response (auto-saved).

Response: messages[] array in chronological order.
- role 'bot': render activities[] using the same code as a /turn response
- role 'user': render text as user bubble
- Last entry is always role 'bot' — the current state awaiting input

Always returns at least one bot entry — if the user opened the chat but had not yet picked a topic, returns the initial greeting + category menu (same as /sessions/create).

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Sends a user action and receives the bot's next activities.

`{{session_id}}` — from POST /sessions/create/create response (auto-saved).

**Action types and when to use each:**

`select_choice` — user taps a quick-reply button:
```json
{ "action": "select_choice", "choice_id": "<id from choices[]>", "user_says": "<button label>" }
```

`send_message` — user types free text (only when disable_input is false):
```json
{ "action": "send_message", "text": "user typed this" }
```

`pick_item` — user selects from a dynamic list (course picker, etc.):
```json
{ "action": "pick_item", "picker_id": "<from picker activity>", "item_id": "<id>", "item_label": "<label>" }
```

**choice_id and picker_id always come from the previous response's activities[]** — never hardcode them.

Repeat until `activities[]` contains `{ "type": "end" }` — that means the conversation is complete.

item.name: Get session trace — NOT YET WIRED (501)

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Returns the full conversation trace (every node visited, branch decisions, API calls). Not yet implemented — returns HTTP 501.

`{{session_id}}` — from POST /sessions/create/create response.

item.name: Delete session data (DPDP) — NOT YET WIRED (501)

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Deletes all personal data for a session (DPDP / right-to-erasure). Not yet implemented — returns HTTP 501.

`{{session_id}}` — from POST /sessions/create/create response.

item.name: 2 — Demo: AUTO_LOGOUT_CRASH (complete flow)
item.description:
A verified end-to-end walkthrough of the AUTO_LOGOUT_CRASH flow.

Run steps 1–4 in order. Step 1 auto-saves session_id; all other steps use {{session_id}}.

Full path: Start → pick topic → pick device → follow steps → resolved → END

This flow works fully in local dev with no real user data needed.

exec: // Save session_id — used automatically in Steps 2–4 via {{session_id}}

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Creates a new session.

Response: greeting + 14 topic buttons. session_id is auto-saved to {{session_id}} by the test script.

item.name: Step 2 — Pick category: Application Crashing / Not Loading

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
{{session_id}} — auto-filled from Step 1.
choice_id — taken from Step 1 response, activities[1].choices[].id

Response: bot asks 'Android or iPhone?' with choice_id 'android' and 'ios'.

item.name: Step 3 — Pick topic: Auto logout / App crash

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
{{session_id}} — auto-filled from Step 1.
choice_id — taken from Step 1 response, activities[1].choices[].id

Response: bot asks 'Android or iPhone?' with choice_id 'android' and 'ios'.

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
{{session_id}} — auto-filled from Step 1.
choice_id — taken from Step 2 response, activities[].choices[].id

Alternative: send 'ios' for iPhone-specific steps.

Response: numbered clear-cache steps + 'Has the issue been resolved?' with choices 'yes' and 'no'.

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
{{session_id}} — auto-filled from Step 1.
choice_id — taken from Step 3 response, activities[].choices[].id

Alternative: send 'no' to escalate to a support ticket.

Expected response:
- activities[0]: markdown 'Glad I could help 🙏'
- activities[1]: { "type": "end", "outcome": "self_served" }
- status: 'satisfied'

After this: GET /sessions/list returns null — session is cleared from Redis.

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
{{session_id}} from Step 1.

Run this after Step 4 to see the full conversation thread that was just completed.

Expected: messages[] with 3 bot entries (device question, clear-cache steps, resolved) and 3 user entries (topic selection, Android, yes).

Note: initial greeting (welcome + topic picker) is not included — history starts from first topic selection.

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Optional verification step after the flow ends.

Expected: { "session_id": null } — confirms Redis cleaned up the session on the 'end' node.

If Redis is not running locally, this will also return null (Redis unavailable fallback).

item.name: 3 — Demo: CERTIFICATE_DOWNLOAD — C1 (Not generated)
item.description:
Certificate not generated / not received — happy path where the course is completed and the certificate is available to download.

Requires a real Keycloak JWT (AUTH_DISABLED=false) so the course picker returns the user's actual enrolled courses.

Path: Start → CERTIFICATE_DOWNLOAD → C1 → pick course → resolution steps → resolved → END

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Creates a new session. session_id auto-saved by test script.

Response: greeting + 14 topic quick_reply buttons.

item.name: Step 2 — Pick category: Content Related Issue

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
{{session_id}} auto-filled from Step 1.

Response: 'What's the issue with your certificate?' with two choices:
- C1: Certificate not generated / not received
- C3: Incorrect name on certificate

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
{{session_id}} auto-filled from Step 1.

Response: 'What's the issue with your certificate?' with two choices:
- C1: Certificate not generated / not received
- C3: Incorrect name on certificate

item.name: Step 4 — Select issue: Certificate not generated (C1)

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Response: course picker asking which course has the certificate issue.

activities[1] will be type 'picker' with:
- picker_id: 'collected.c1_course_id'  ← use this in Step 4
- items[]: list of user's enrolled courses (requires real JWT)

Each item in items[]: { id, label } — pick one for Step 4.

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
picker_id: always 'collected.c1_course_id' for this flow.
item_id + item_label: copy from Step 3 response → activities[1].items[n].id and .label

The server checks enrollment status and branches:
- Course completed + 24h elapsed → shows download steps → go to Step 5
- Course completed + < 24h → 'Wait 24 hours' → END
- Course in-progress → shows pending resources → go to Step 5
- Course not started → shows completion steps → go to Step 5
- Course not found → re-shows picker (repeat Step 4 with correct item)

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
choice_id from Step 4 response → last activity's choices[].id (yes / no)

Send 'yes' → status: satisfied, type: end, outcome: self_served
Send 'no' → LLM auto-raises a Zoho support ticket → confirm_ticket activity → END

item.name: 4 — Demo: CERTIFICATE_DOWNLOAD — C3 (Incorrect name)
item.description:
Incorrect name on certificate — the bot fetches the user's current profile name, lets them correct it, and updates it via the Karmayogi API.

Requires a real Keycloak JWT (AUTH_DISABLED=false) for the course picker and profile API calls.

Path: Start → CERTIFICATE_DOWNLOAD → C3 → pick course → see current name → enter new name → confirm → API update → resolved → END

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description: Creates a new session. session_id auto-saved by test script.

item.name: Step 2 — Pick category: Content Related Issue

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description: Response: 'What's the issue?' with C1 and C3 buttons.

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description: Response: 'What's the issue?' with C1 and C3 buttons.

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Response: course picker asking which course has the wrong name.

activities[1]: type 'picker' with:
- picker_id: 'collected.c3_course_id'  ← use in Step 4
- items[]: user's completed enrolled courses

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
picker_id: always 'collected.c3_course_id' for this flow.
item_id + item_label: copy from Step 3 → activities[1].items[n]

The server fetches the user's current profile name from Karmayogi and returns:
'Your certificate for <course> was generated with the name: <First> <Last>. Would you like to update this name?'
- choices: yes / no

Send 'yes' → continue to Step 5 (name correction)
Send 'no' → END ('No problem, name stays unchanged')

item.name: Step 6 — Confirm: Yes, update the name

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Response contains TWO activities — this is the input type pattern:
```json
[
  { "type": "markdown", "content": "Please enter your correct **First Name** (as per government ID):" },
  { "type": "input", "input_id": "collected.c3_new_first_name", "input_placeholder": "" }
]
```

Rendering rule for `type: input`:
1. Render the preceding `markdown` activity as a chat bubble (the question)
2. Render the `input` activity as a free-text input box
3. When the user submits → send `send_message` with `text: <what they typed>` (Step 6)

`input_id` tells you which field is being collected — useful if you want to apply client-side validation (e.g. email pattern check before sending).

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Responding to the `type: input` activity from Step 5.

`action: send_message` is the correct action whenever the previous response contained `type: input`. The `text` field is whatever the user typed in the input box.

Response: another markdown + input pair asking for the Last Name (same pattern as Step 5 — repeat with Step 7).

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Responding to the `type: input` activity from Step 6.

Same pattern as Step 6 — `action: send_message`, `text` is the user's last name.

Response: name confirmation with `quick_replies`:
'Change certificate name from: <old> to: Ramesh Kumar. Shall we proceed?'
- choices: yes (confirm) / no (re-enter name)

Send 'yes' → Step 8 (API update)
Send 'no' → bot re-asks for first name (repeat from Step 6)

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Triggers PATCH /api/user/private/v1/update on Karmayogi with the new name.

On success: '✅ Your name has been updated to Ramesh Kumar. To re-download certificate: go to Profile → My Learning → Completed → find course → Download Certificate'
Then asks: 'Was the name correction successful?' → yes/no

On API failure: bot raises a Zoho support ticket automatically.

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
choice_id from Step 8 response → activities[last].choices[].id

Send 'yes' → status: satisfied, type: end, outcome: self_served
Send 'no' → LLM auto-raises Zoho ticket with course name, old name, new name → confirm_ticket → END

item.name: 5 — Demo: TICKET RAISED (issue unresolved → auto-ticket)
item.description:
Shows what happens when the user says the issue is NOT resolved — the LLM automatically raises a Zoho support ticket and the session ends with outcome: ticket_raised.

This is the most important terminal state to handle in the UI: the response will contain a ticket_id and the frontend should display a 'Ticket raised' confirmation.

Path: Start → AUTO_LOGOUT_CRASH → Android → No (not resolved) → LLM raises ticket → ticket_id in response → END

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description: session_id auto-saved by test script.

item.name: Step 2 — Pick category: Application Crashing / Not Loading

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description: Response: 'Android or iPhone?' with choice_id 'android' and 'ios'.

item.name: Step 3 — Select topic: Auto logout / App crash

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description: Response: 'Android or iPhone?' with choice_id 'android' and 'ios'.

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description: Response: clear-cache steps + 'Has the issue been resolved?' with choice_id 'yes' and 'no'.

item.name: Step 5 — Select: No, issue still persists → ticket raised

exec: // When outcome is ticket_raised, ticket_id will be the Zoho ticket number

header.description: Kong API gateway JWT — required when calling through Kong (dev/UAT/prod)

request.description:
Choosing 'no' triggers the LLM ticket path:
1. LLM generates ticket subject + description from conversation context
2. Zoho ticket created via API
3. Bot confirms: '✅ Ticket #T12345 raised. L2 team will reach you within 2 business days.'
4. Session ends with type: 'end', outcome: 'ticket_raised'

Expected response shape:
```json
{
  "session_id": "...",
  "ticket_id": "T12345",       ← Zoho ticket number — show this in the UI
  "activities": [
    { "type": "markdown", "content": "✅ Ticket #T12345 raised..." },
    { "type": "end", "outcome": "ticket_raised" }
  ],
  "status": "ticket_raised"
}
```

UI must handle two terminal outcomes differently:
- outcome: 'self_served' → show 'Issue resolved' banner
- outcome: 'ticket_raised' → show 'Ticket #<ticket_id> raised' banner with 2-day SLA note

If Zoho API fails: outcome becomes 'ticket_failed' with a message to email support directly.
